title: Guard datasets[0].data access in JS chart datalabels/tooltip callbacks
analysis:
Both memory_graphs.html and performance.html contain doughnut chart configurations with datalabels formatter and tooltip label callbacks that access `ctx.chart.data.datasets[0].data.reduce(...)` without guarding against an empty `datasets` array. There are 4 call sites total:

1. memory_graphs.html line 121 — datalabels formatter (shared by memoryOpsChart and memoryAgentChart)
2. memory_graphs.html line 130 — tooltip label callback (same charts)
3. performance.html line 168 — datalabels formatter (pathChart doughnut)
4. performance.html line 177 — tooltip label callback (same chart)

If a chart renders with no data (empty dashboard, HTMX partial re-render), `datasets[0]` is undefined and `.data.reduce()` throws TypeError.

Fix pattern: Extract `var ds = ctx.chart.data.datasets[0]; if (!ds) return '';` before each `.data.reduce()` call, then use `ds.data.reduce(...)` instead.

Existing test infrastructure uses the FastAPI TestClient with mocked data functions, rendering the templates and asserting on the HTML output. Tests can verify the guard pattern is present in the rendered JavaScript by checking for the guard idiom in the HTML string.

steps.description: Write failing tests in TestDoughnutDatalabels (test_memory_graphs_partial.py) that assert the datalabels formatter and tooltip callback contain a dataset emptiness guard (e.g., 'if (!ds)' pattern). Add two new test methods: test_datalabels_formatter_guards_empty_datasets and test_tooltip_callback_guards_empty_datasets.

In memory_graphs.html, add the guard `var ds = ctx.chart.data.datasets[0]; if (!ds) return '';` in the datalabels formatter (line 121) and tooltip label callback (line 130). Replace `ctx.chart.data.datasets[0].data.reduce(...)` with `ds.data.reduce(...)` in both locations.

Write failing tests in TestCompletionPathsDatalabels (test_performance_panel.py) that assert the datalabels formatter and tooltip callback contain a dataset emptiness guard. Add two new test methods: test_datalabels_formatter_guards_empty_datasets and test_tooltip_callback_guards_empty_datasets.

In performance.html, add the guard `var ds = ctx.chart.data.datasets[0]; if (!ds) return '';` in the datalabels formatter (line 168) and tooltip label callback (line 177). Replace `ctx.chart.data.datasets[0].data.reduce(...)` with `ds.data.reduce(...)` in both locations.

design_decisions.decision: Use `var ds = ctx.chart.data.datasets[0]; if (!ds) return '';` as the guard pattern
design_decisions.rationale: This is the exact pattern specified in the task description. It's concise, handles the undefined case by returning an empty string (which Chart.js treats as 'no label'), and avoids touching the reduce logic itself.

design_decisions.decision: Test via HTML string matching on the rendered template output rather than browser-level JS execution
design_decisions.rationale: All existing tests in this suite use the same pattern — render the template via TestClient and assert on the HTML string. This is consistent with the codebase convention and avoids needing a headless browser. The guard pattern is distinctive enough (e.g., 'if (!ds)') to reliably match.

design_decisions.decision: Keep both datalabels and tooltip guards in each template as separate assertions, not a single combined test
design_decisions.rationale: The formatter and tooltip callback are separate code paths that could independently regress. Separate test methods give clear failure signals about which callback is affected.

reuse.how: Reuse the existing mock-patching helper to render memory_graphs.html with test data, same as all other tests in that file.
Reuse the existing ExitStack-based mock helper to render performance.html with test data, same as all other tests in that file.
Add new test methods to these existing classes rather than creating new test classes, since they already group datalabels-related assertions.
Standard FastAPI TestClient fixture used by all dashboard tests.
